metadata.description: Skills for the LLM Wiki: author processor-ready source docs and convert SOPs into specs.

plugins.name: wiki-doc-author
plugins.description: Produce the source docs an app feeds into the LLM Wiki (processor) — a compliant README for any kind (API / cronjob / worker / knowledge), plus a fresh openapi.json for OpenAPI-capable apps. Self-contained, bundled stdlib tools.

plugins.name: sop-to-spec
plugins.description: Convert an operations SOP (DBA runbook, infra procedure, deployment checklist) into a spec a human can approve and an AI agent can implement a three-layer FastAPI service from.

plugins.name: skill-author
plugins.description: Add or modify an installable Claude Code skill in this repo — produce a spec-compliant SKILL.md, bundle its scripts/references, and register it in the marketplace. Use when authoring a new skill or editing an existing one.

plugins.name: llm-wiki-skills
plugins.description: Bundle — installs every LLM Wiki skill at once (wiki-doc-author + sop-to-spec + skill-author).

plugins.name: superpowers
plugins.description: External — brainstorming, subagent-driven dev with code review, systematic debugging, red/green TDD, and skill authoring. Mirrored from obra/superpowers into the internal GitLab; swap the url to your GitLab mirror. No sha = follows the mirror's default branch, so `/plugin marketplace update` pulls whatever the mirror last synced.

plugins.name: andrej-karpathy-skills
plugins.description: External — behavioral guidelines to reduce common LLM coding mistakes (Think Before Coding, Simplicity, …). Mirrored from forrestchang/andrej-karpathy-skills into the internal GitLab; swap the url to your GitLab mirror. No sha = follows the mirror's default branch.
